evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Pick app, change the highlight to a rectangle that's orange while an item still needs picking and green once it's picked. File: IntegratedBarcodePickViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill sets a BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleRectangular on the existing BarcodePickViewSettings (before the BarcodePickView is constructed), with a per-state brush — an orange-ish brush for .toPick and a green-ish brush for .picked — via setBrush(_:for:). The existing integration (context, settings, product provider, mode, view, listeners) is preserved.

assertions.text: A BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleRectangular is constructed
setBrush(_:for:) is called with .toPick and a Brush whose color is orange (or a clearly orange-ish UIColor)
setBrush(_:for:) is called with .picked and a Brush whose color is green
The style is assigned to the existing viewSettings.highlightStyle BEFORE the BarcodePickView(frame:context:barcodePick:settings:) constructor is called
No custom-view or delegate-based styling is introduced (a plain Rectangular style with brushes is sufficient for this request)

Existing integration is preserved: DataCaptureContext, BarcodePickSettings + symbologies, BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProvider, BarcodePick mode, BarcodePickView construction, the scanning listener, action listener, and UI delegate are all left intact (no duplicate mode/view/context)

evals.prompt: Show an icon on each MatrixScan Pick highlight — a to-pick icon while the item still needs picking, and a checkmark once it's picked. File: IntegratedBarcodePickViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill switches the highlight to one of the icon-capable styles (BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleDotWithIcons or BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleRectangularWithIcons) and sets per-state icons using the ScanditIcon API: setScanditIcon(...) with the .toPick icon for .toPick, and setSelectedScanditIcon(...) with the .checkmark icon for .picked. Icons are built with ScanditIconBuilder and given an explicit withIconColor (since the default glyph color is white). The existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: An icon-capable style is used: BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleDotWithIcons or BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleRectangularWithIcons
setScanditIcon(_:for:) is called for .toPick with a ScanditIcon built via ScanditIconBuilder().withIcon(.toPick) (the built-in ScanditIcon API is preferred over a UIImage)
setSelectedScanditIcon(_:for:) is called for .picked with a ScanditIcon built via ScanditIconBuilder().withIcon(.checkmark)
Each ScanditIcon sets an explicit withIconColor (not left at the default, which renders white/invisible)
The style is assigned to the existing viewSettings.highlightStyle before the BarcodePickView is constructed
Existing integration is preserved (context, settings, provider, mode, view, listeners) — no duplicate mode/view/context

evals.prompt: Draw a custom view over each barcode in my MatrixScan Pick app showing the product identifier. File: IntegratedBarcodePickViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill uses BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleCustomView with a delegate. The view controller conforms to BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleCustomViewDelegate and implements customView(for:completionHandler:), building a UIView (e.g. a UILabel) from request.productIdentifier and returning it via BarcodePickHighlightCustomViewResponse(view:statusIconStyle:). The style's delegate is set and assigned to viewSettings.highlightStyle. The existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: A BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleCustomView is constructed and its delegate is set

The view controller conforms to BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleCustomViewDelegate and implements customView(for:completionHandler:) — note the Swift selector is customView(for:), not customViewForRequest:
The delegate builds a UIView from request.productIdentifier (e.g. a UILabel whose text is the product identifier)

The delegate returns the view via BarcodePickHighlightCustomViewResponse(view:statusIconStyle:) — the statusIconStyle: argument must be passed explicitly (nil is fine), not omitted, since it is a required parameter
The style is assigned to the existing viewSettings.highlightStyle before the BarcodePickView is constructed
Existing integration is preserved (context, settings, provider, mode, view, listeners) — no duplicate mode/view/context

evals.prompt: Show a status badge on each MatrixScan Pick highlight with the product identifier as its text. File: IntegratedBarcodePickViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill uses an icon-capable style (BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleDotWithIcons or RectangularWithIcons), sets the style's delegate, and implements BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleDelegate.style(for:completionHandler:) returning a BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleResponse whose statusIconStyle is a BarcodePickStatusIconStyle carrying request.productIdentifier as text. The badge must be visible (not white-on-white). Existing integration preserved.

assertions.text: An icon-capable style is used (BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleDotWithIcons or BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleRectangularWithIcons) and assigned to viewSettings.highlightStyle before the view is constructed
The style's delegate is set (e.g. style.delegate = self) so the per-barcode status icon is actually supplied
The view controller conforms to BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleDelegate and implements style(for:completionHandler:) — Swift selector style(for:), not styleForRequest:
The returned BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleResponse includes a statusIconStyle (a BarcodePickStatusIconStyle) whose text is request.productIdentifier

The status icon is constructed so it is visible — either BarcodePickStatusIconStyle(iconColor:iconBackgroundColor:text:) with a non-white iconBackgroundColor, or a ScanditIcon whose withIconColor is non-white (a white icon on the white badge background would be invisible)
Existing integration is preserved (context, settings, provider, mode, view, listeners) — no duplicate mode/view/context

evals.prompt: Add a low-stock warning status badge (using the built-in Scandit low-stock icon) to each MatrixScan Pick highlight. File: IntegratedBarcodePickViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill uses an icon-capable style with statusIconSettings, sets the style delegate, and returns a BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleResponse with a statusIconStyle built from BarcodePickStatusIconStyle(scanditIcon:text:), where the ScanditIcon is ScanditIconBuilder().withIcon(.lowStock) with an explicit non-white withIconColor (the status badge background is white, so a white glyph would be invisible). Existing integration preserved.

assertions.text: An icon-capable style (DotWithIcons or RectangularWithIcons) is used with a BarcodePickStatusIconSettings assigned to statusIconSettings
The style delegate returns a statusIconStyle built with BarcodePickStatusIconStyle(scanditIcon:text:)
The ScanditIcon uses ScanditIconBuilder().withIcon(.lowStock)
The ScanditIcon sets an explicit non-white withIconColor — a white glyph on the white status-badge background would be invisible (this is the key correctness point)
The style is assigned to viewSettings.highlightStyle before the BarcodePickView is constructed
Existing integration is preserved (context, settings, provider, mode, view, listeners) — no duplicate mode/view/context

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Pick app, customize the highlights per barcode through a delegate: if the scanned barcode's data is an even number, show a checkmark icon on the highlight; if it's odd, show the highlight with no icon. File: IntegratedBarcodePickViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill uses an icon-capable style (BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleDotWithIcons or RectangularWithIcons) with its delegate set, and implements BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleDelegate.style(for:completionHandler:). Inside, it parses request.itemData (the barcode payload) to decide even vs odd, and returns a BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleResponse that includes an icon (a ScanditIcon with a non-white withIconColor) when even and no icon (nil icon) when odd. Both branches still provide a visible brush so the highlight shape is drawn in either case. Existing integration preserved.

assertions.text: An icon-capable style (BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleDotWithIcons or RectangularWithIcons) is used, its delegate is set, and it is assigned to viewSettings.highlightStyle before the view is constructed
The view controller conforms to BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleDelegate and implements style(for:completionHandler:) (Swift selector style(for:), not styleForRequest:)
The delegate parses request.itemData as a number to branch on even vs odd (e.g. Int(request.itemData).map { $0 % 2 == 0 })
In the even branch the response includes an icon (a ScanditIcon built via ScanditIconBuilder, e.g. .checkmark) with an explicit non-white withIconColor
In the odd branch the response includes no icon (nil scanditIcon/icon) — and still passes a visible (non-nil) brush so the highlight shape is drawn rather than transparent
The returned type is BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleResponse via one of its real initializers (brush:scanditIcon:statusIconStyle: or brush:icon:statusIconStyle:), not a guessed type or initializer
Existing integration is preserved (context, settings, provider, mode, view, listeners) — no duplicate mode/view/context
